gq 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference
    • Getting started with gq

Merge multiple gq registries

Source: R/gq_reg.R
gq_reg_merge.Rd

Takes any number of registry list objects and merges their layers into a single master registry. For duplicate layer keys, the priority argument controls which source wins. Conflicts are logged as an attribute.

Usage

gq_reg_merge(..., csv = NULL, priority = c("last", "first"))

Arguments

...

Registry list objects (from gq_reg_read(), gq_reg_read_csv(), or gq_registry_read()).

csv

Optional character vector of CSV file paths to include. Each is read via gq_reg_read_csv() and appended to the merge inputs.

priority

Either "last" (default, later sources win) or "first" (earlier sources win) for duplicate layer keys.

Value

A merged registry list. Conflicts are stored in a "conflicts" attribute (a data.frame with columns: layer_key, source_a, source_b).

Examples

path <- system.file("examples", "mini_registry.json", package = "gq")
reg1 <- gq_reg_read(path)
reg2 <- gq_reg_read(path)
merged <- gq_reg_merge(reg1, reg2)
length(merged$layers)
#> [1] 4
